value.claim: ViLBERT is a two-stream visiolinguistic model that extends BERT with co-attentional transformer layers to learn task-agnostic joint representations of image content and natural language.

The proposed pretraining tasks (masked multi-modal modelling and multi-modal alignment prediction) on the Conceptual Captions dataset enable effective visual grounding without task-specific fine-tuning initially.

ViLBERT achieves state-of-the-art performance on four vision-and-language tasks: Visual Question Answering (VQA 2.0), Visual Commonsense Reasoning (VCR), Referring Expressions (RefCOCO+), and Caption-Based Image Retrieval (Flickr30k).
The two-stream architecture with sparse co-attention outperforms single-stream unified models and models without the specific pretraining objectives across multiple downstream tasks.

Pretrained ViLBERT can perform zero-shot caption-based image retrieval on Flickr30k, demonstrating that visual grounding capabilities are learned during pretraining and generalize without fine-tuning.
